output.dependency: The measure group/category determines the structure and prefix of the measure code. In the sample, the category 'surgical infection prevention' corresponds to codes of the form 'scip-inf-*'.
The measure code determines the full measure description. Each coded identifier corresponds to a specific textual explanation of what is being measured.
The composite identifier is syntactically built from the state abbreviation and the measure code. In the sample, the last column follows the pattern '<state>_<measure code>', e.g. 'al_scip-inf-4'.
The state abbreviation forms the prefix of the composite identifier in the last column. In the sample, 'al' appears as the leading segment before the underscore.
The facility identifier appears to determine the facility name. The same numeric identifier is associated with the same hospital name across the records shown.
The facility identifier appears to determine the street address. The same numeric identifier is associated with the same street location in the sample.
The facility identifier appears to determine the city. The same facility identifier is paired with the same city across the records shown.
The facility identifier appears to determine the state abbreviation. The same facility identifier is paired with the same state in the sample.
The facility identifier appears to determine the ZIP code. The same facility identifier is associated with the same postal code in the sample.
The facility identifier appears to determine the county name. The same facility identifier is paired with the same county across the records shown.
The facility identifier appears to determine the telephone number. The same facility identifier is associated with the same phone number in the sample.
The facility identifier appears to determine the facility type/category. In the sample, the same identifier maps to the same hospital type.
The facility identifier appears to determine the ownership classification. The same facility identifier is paired with the same ownership description across the records shown.
The facility name appears semantically linked to the street address; the same named institution is shown at the same address in the sample.
The city is semantically dependent on the state abbreviation in the sense that the listed city belongs to that state. In the sample, 'fort payne' is in 'al'.
The city appears related to the ZIP code; the same city is shown with the same postal code in the sample.
The state abbreviation is related to the ZIP code; the ZIP shown belongs to that state in the sample.

The performance/result value is related to the patient-count text. Special result forms can correspond to count patterns; in the sample, a missing/empty-style result is paired with '0 patients', while '100%' is paired with a positive patient count.
